urgency.message: 4 critical CVE(s) and 9 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: SAP fixes critical flaws in NetWeaver and Commerce Cloud
items.summary: SAP has released fixes for 15 vulnerabilities as part of its June 2026 Security Patch package, including four critical-severity flaws affecting SAP NetWeaver and SAP Commerce Cloud. [...]

items.title: Miasma Supply Chain Worm Burrows Into 73 Microsoft Repositories
items.summary: The attacks stemmed from a GitHub account that was also compromised in a previous Miasmi attack on Microsoft last month.

items.title: Microsoft Patches 200 Vulnerabilities
items.summary:
Three of the vulnerabilities fixed with the latest Patch Tuesday updates were publicly disclosed before Microsoft addressed them.
The post Microsoft Patches 200 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA to transform how it assesses cyber vulnerabilities and risks, Andersen says
items.summary: A binding operational directive being released Wednesday will direct federal agencies to change the way they address vulnerabilities by elevating some while putting others to the side.

items.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5094127 extended security update
items.summary: Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5094127 extended security update, which fixes the June 2026 Patch Tuesday vulnerabilities and adds new functionality to monitor the rollout of updated Secure Boot certificates that replace those expiring this month. [...]

items.title: Adobe Patches 123 Vulnerabilities
items.summary:
Nearly half of the security holes, most allowing arbitrary code execution, have been fixed in Adobe’s Experience Manager product.
The post Adobe Patches 123 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft’s June 2026 Patch Tuesday Addresses 198 CVEs ( CVE-2026-49160, CVE-2026-50507)
items.summary: 32Critical166Important0Moderate0LowMicrosoft addresses 198 CVEs in the largest Patch Tuesday release, including three zero-days.Microsoft patched 198 CVEs in its June 2026 Patch Tuesday release, with 32 rated critical and 166 rated as important. Our counts omitted 6 CVEs that were already addressed

items.title: Microsoft June 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes 3 zero-day, 200 flaws
items.summary: Today is Microsoft's June 2026 Patch Tuesday, with security updates for 200 flaws and three publicly disclosed zero-day vulnerabilities. [...]

items.title: Microsoft June 2026 Patch Tuesday, (Tue, Jun 9th)
items.summary: Microsoft today released patches for 204 vulnerabilities. 38 of these vulnerabilities are considered critical, and three have been disclosed before today. Six of the vulnerabilities affect Microsoft cloud solutions and do not require any user action. In addition, Microsoft incorporated 360 different

items.title: Windows 11 KB5094126 & KB5093998 cumulative updates released
items.summary: Microsoft has released Windows 11 KB5094126 and KB5093998 cumulative updates for versions 25H2/24H2 and 23H2 to fix security vulnerabilities, bugs, and add new features. [...]

items.title: Meta to Use Off-Site Business Data for Feed and AI Personalization
items.summary:
Meta on Tuesday announced that it will use information shared by other businesses to personalize users' feed and responses from its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot, expanding its scope beyond targeted ads.

"Businesses often share information about people's activity on their sites with us to ma

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Fable 5: Mythos-Class AI With Cybersecurity Guardrails
items.summary:
The AI giant also announced that Project Glasswing partners are being given access to the upgraded Mythos 5.
The post Anthropic Launches Claude Fable 5: Mythos-Class AI With Cybersecurity Guardrails  appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Anthropic Offers Mythos Upgrade for Cyber Partners and a ‘Safe’ Version for the Rest of You
items.summary: Anthropic is releasing Claude Mythos 5 to trusted organizations and Claude Fable 5 to the public, a version it says can’t be used for cyberattacks.

items.title: OpenSSL Patches High-Severity Vulnerability Found With AI
items.summary:
A total of 18 vulnerabilities have been patched in the latest OpenSSL releases, including many that were potentially discovered by AI.
The post OpenSSL Patches High-Severity Vulnerability Found With AI appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Veeam Backup & Replication RCE Flaw Lets Domain Users Run Remote Code
items.summary:
Veeam has released security patches to address a critical flaw in its Backup & Replication software that could result in remote code execution.

Tracked as CVE-2026-44963, the vulnerability carries a CVSS score of 9.4 out of a maximum of 10.0.

"A vulnerability allowing remote code execution (RC

items.title: Microsoft Restores Some GitHub Repos, Keeps Others Offline as Miasma Probe Continues
items.summary:
Microsoft on Monday confirmed that it temporarily removed some GitHub repositories in response to a recent security incident that led to 73 of its open-source projects being compromised to inject an information stealer into the code.

"Our priority is to protect customers and the broader ecosystem,"

items.title: XBOW tests Anthropic's Mythos Preview for offensive security
items.summary: Anthropic's Mythos Preview was highly effective at finding vulnerability candidates, especially when analyzing source code. XBOW explores how the model performed across exploit discovery, reverse engineering, and live-site validation. [...]

items.title: GitHub disables Microsoft repos pushing password-stealing malware
items.summary: Microsoft removed 73 repositories across its Azure, microsoft, Azure-Samples, and MicrosoftDocs organizations on GitHub, disrupting continuous integration pipelines. [...]

items.title: Russian Attackers Weaponize WinRAR Flaw Against Ukrainian Orgs
items.summary: Two separate campaigns target CVE-2025-8088, fixed last July, to conduct data theft and cyberespionage against military and government targets in Ukraine.

items.title: 75% of Firms Deploy Vulnerable Code Amid Pressure on CISOs, Report Finds
items.summary: Checkmarx report warns that business pressure is among the reason security leaders let security compliance slip

items.title: Claude Mythos Turns N-Days Into N-Hours With Rapid Exploit Creation
items.summary:
Public LLM models with safeguards turned off can also build working exploits, increasing patch gap risks.
The post Claude Mythos Turns N-Days Into N-Hours With Rapid Exploit Creation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: AI Coding Adoption Hits 97% but Governance Lags Behind
items.summary: Most dev teams use AI coding assistants but only 30% have full governance in place

items.title: New Veeam vulnerability exposes backup servers to RCE attacks
items.summary: Veeam has released security updates to patch a critical Backup & Replication security flaw that can be exploited to gain remote code execution (RCE) on domain-joined backup servers. [...]

items.title: CVE-2026-21530 Windows Rich Text Edit Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability
items.summary: Added Office softwares to the Security Updates table. Customers that are running supported versions of Office are encouraged to update to the indicated versions to be protected from this vulnerability.

items.title: CVE-2026-45585 Windows BitLocker Security Feature Bypass Vulnerability
items.summary: Updated product information in the Software Update table. This is an informational change only.

items.title: CVE-2024-49075 Windows Remote Desktop Services Denial of Service Vulnerability
items.summary: To comprehensively address this vulnerability Windows 11 Version 26H1 for ARM64-based Systems and Windows 11 Version 26H1 for64-based Systems have been added to the Security Updates table. Microsoft recommend updating to the June 2026 version of your Windows operating systems.

items.title: CVE-2024-49123 Windows Remote Desktop Services Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
items.summary: To comprehensively address this vulnerability Windows 11 Version 26H1 for ARM64-based Systems and Windows 11 Version 26H1 for64-based Systems have been added to the Security Updates table. Microsoft recommend updating to the June 2026 version of your Windows operating systems.

items.summary: To comprehensively address this vulnerability Windows 11 Version 26H1 for ARM64-based Systems and Windows 11 Version 26H1 for64-based Systems have been added to the Security Updates table. Microsoft recommend updating to the June 2026 version of your Windows operating systems.

items.title: Critical phpBB Flaw Lets Attackers Hijack Any Account with One Request
items.summary: Critical phpBB authentication bypass lets attackers hijack any account with one request

items.title: Hackers pose as women seeking romance to spy on Russian soldiers
items.summary: The group, dubbed SiribClone by Russian cybersecurity firm F6, has been active since at least the summer of 2025 and has primarily targeted members of the Russian armed forces stationed in border regions and combat zones.

items.title: New Platform Uses Cryptographic Invisibility to Protect AI-Built Applications
items.summary:
Atsign’s AI Architect applies cryptographic protections to agentic software development, aiming to prevent attackers from exploiting vulnerabilities by making application identities effectively invisible.

items.title: WinRAR Flaw Exploited by Russia-Aligned Groups to Deploy Stealers in Ukraine
items.summary:
Two Russia-aligned cyber attack campaigns have continued to exploit a security flaw in WinRAR to target Ukrainian organisations, almost a year after patches for the vulnerability were released.

The flaws could lead to the disclosure of sensitive information, memory corruption, and disruption of normal system usage.

items.title: Schneider Electric EcoStruxure Panel Server
items.summary:
View CSAF
Summary
Schneider Electric is aware of its vulnerability in its EcoStruxure Panel Server offer. The EcoStruxure Panel Server is a high performance, modular gateway with enhanced cybersecurity that provides easy and fast connections to multiple concurrent edge control or cloud applications.

items.summary:

items.title: New FROST Attack Lets Websites Track What Sites and Apps You Open via SSD Timing
items.summary:
A malicious website can work out which sites you visit and which apps you open, using nothing but JavaScript and the timing of your SSD. The attack, called FROST, needs no native code, no extension, and no permission prompt.
